title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple discusses her interest in celebrity gossip magazines with Shelley, explaining that she studies them to understand human nature. Their conversation turns to the death of Heather Badcock. Shelley notes that while the police have not officially declared it a murder, the circumstances do not suggest suicide. She describes Badcock as a high-energy "busybody" and observes that while Badcock's husband, Arthur, appeared depressed at a recent party, he now seems more lively. Shelley suggests the death might have been an accident involving a contaminated drink, a theory Miss Marple finds plausible but approaches with caution.

Detective Chief Inspector Demott Craddock visits Miss Marple to share information regarding the case. He provides a list of individuals who were present on the landing at the time of Badcock's death. While many locals and officials have been ruled out, the investigation continues to focus on husbands. Both Cridland and Miss Marple suspect that the intended victim of the crime was actually Marina Gregg, rather than Heather Badcock. Motivated by the reputation of Miss Marple’s former "Tuesday Night Club" for solving mysteries, Cridland seeks her assistance in analyzing the witnesses and their locations. In response, Miss Marple shifts the focus of the investigation toward Marina Gregg’s adopted children—a local child, a foreign refugee, and an American child—seeking information regarding their current whereabouts.
